feedback.text: As a consumer, I cannot understand how by paying the price that the producer has determined that his product is worth he cannot make use of and enjoy it at the time I decide, whether it is today or within 30 years. As a video game developer I would also not want my work to be lost and my entire invested life would not have served anything. I think this initiative does not help consumers themselves, but also creators who invest many hours of their lives in a dream. In my view, determining the lifespan of a programme is identical to the planned obsolescence of everyday items, and the sole objective that businesses have is to channel the movement of consumers to buy the most current. I can still play video games 10 years ago and the only problem is to reconcile current technology with the previous one, but this is a consumer problem.
